attacking_style: Launches solo attacks from breakaways at 10km to go on summit finishes, preferring long-range efforts (up to 87km) where superior descending and race management eliminate collaboration. Targets fragmentation points on steep gradients rather than explosive late surges.
team_tactics: Transitioned from protected GC rider (2017-2018 Bora) to elite domestique (2022 UAE), executing high-pace leadouts on extreme gradients (11-20%) to destroy peloton structure. Team positioning extends to flat stages protecting GC placement, not just mountain support.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained climbs with tactical complexity allowing solo management; strong descender. Struggles on ultra-steep finishes (14%+) where power-to-weight becomes limiting factor. Time trials remain persistent weakness, especially sustained 10km efforts.
vulnerability: Exposed on 14%+ gradient finishes despite climbing pedigree; time trial efforts consistently costly (16+ seconds gaps, fades after strong starts). Tactical awareness lapses when monitoring rivals (failed to anticipate attacks despite watching). Crash-prone on decisive stages (3 GC-ending crashes observed).
key_patterns: Solo breakaway victories from 10-87km out, preferring race management over collaboration (2 wins observed)
Effective on 11-20% gradients in domestique role, destroying peloton structure for team leaders
Crashes on decisive stages eliminated GC contention multiple times across different seasons
Time trial weakness consistent: fades in sustained efforts, loses podium positions to specialists
Tactical passivity when rivals attack despite strong positioning, defensive mindset costs opportunities
form_trajectory: neutral: Role evolved from GC contender to elite domestique, executing world-class pacing on extreme gradients (2022 TDF/UAE Tour) but tactical ambitions appear subordinated to team duty
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15
